LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - WEN LE CHEN'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 3, 2014 BECAUSE OF JUDGE GOLDSMITH'S RECUSAL TO HEAR THE MATTER. WELLS FARGO IS THE CREDITOR. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: ANGIE DINER, CSR # 9581

LAW AND MOTION 302, ASSIGNEE SUNLAN LDP, LLC'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF WEN LIE CHEN. WEN LIE CHEN'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS DENIED IN PART. CLAIMANT WEN LIE CHEN HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT ALL FUNDS ARE NECESSARY FOR THE SUPPORT OF THE CLAIMANT, SPOUSE AND FAMILY. PLAINTIFF'S JUDGMENT TAKES PRIORITY OVER OTHER DEBT, AND THE CLAIMED TRANSPORTATION EXPENSES ARE EXCESSIVE IN LIGHT OF THE PENDING JUDGMENT. WAGES WILL BE GARNISHED IN THE AMOUNT OF $300 PER PAY PERIOD. (THE COURT WILL PREPARE THE ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR# 12353.

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption And Ntc Of Oppos
Off Calendar Mar-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Wen Chen, Tel. 626.688.1344, defendant/judgment debtor/claimant appearing on his own behalf, together with Ms. Fang Rong, Mandarin interpreter;
No other appearance.

RULING: Mr. Chen did not notify the moving party that he will appear to contest the tentative ruling. Therefore, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on ASSIGNEE SUNLAN LDP, LLC's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption of Wen Lie Chen. Wen Lie Chen's claim of exemption is denied in part. Claimant Wen Lie Chen has not demonstrated that all funds are necessary for the support of the claimant, spouse and family. Plaintiff's judgment takes priority over other debt, and the claimed transportation expenses are excessive in light of the pending judgment. Wages will be garnished in the amount of $300 per pay period. (The Court will prepare the order.) Judge: Peter Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR# 12353.

Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption And Ntc Of Oppos
Off Calendar Feb-27-2014 Continued To Mar-03-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JILL BATES-MOORE, TEL. NO. (510) 985-9250, SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
CHEN WEN LE, TEL. NO. (626) 688-1344, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER WITH THE HELP OF A
MANDARIN INTERPRETER, FANG RONG.

RULING - WEN LE CHEN'S Hearing On Claim Of Exemption is continued to March 3, 2014 because of Judge Goldsmith's recusal to hear the matter. Wells Fargo is the creditor. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR # 9581
